code.description: Update Login flow using the code method

address.description:
Address is the address to send the code to, in case that there are multiple addresses. This field
is only used in two-factor flows and is ineffective for passwordless flows.

code.description: Code is the 6 digits code sent to the user

csrf_token.description: CSRFToken is the anti-CSRF token

identifier.description:
Identifier is the code identifier
The identifier requires that the user has already completed the registration or settings with code flow.

method.description: Method should be set to "code" when logging in using the code strategy.

resend.description: Resend is set when the user wants to resend the code

transient_payload.description: Transient data to pass along to any webhooks

code.title: updateLoginFlowWithCodeMethod

identifier_first.description: Update Login Flow with Multi-Step Method

csrf_token.description: Sending the anti-csrf token is only required for browser login flows.

identifier.description: Identifier is the email or username of the user trying to log in.

method.description: Method should be set to "password" when logging in using the identifier and password strategy.

transient_payload.description: Transient data to pass along to any webhooks

identifier_first.title: updateLoginFlowWithIdentifierFirstMethod

lookup_secret.description: Update Login Flow with Lookup Secret Method

csrf_token.description: Sending the anti-csrf token is only required for browser login flows.

method.description: Method should be set to "lookup_secret" when logging in using the lookup_secret strategy.

lookup_secret.title: updateLoginFlowWithLookupSecretMethod

oidc.description: Update Login Flow with OpenID Connect Method

csrf_token.description: The CSRF Token

id_token.description:
IDToken is an optional id token provided by an OIDC provider

If submitted, it is verified using the OIDC provider's public key set and the claims are used to populate
the OIDC credentials of the identity.
If the OIDC provider does not store additional claims (such as name, etc.) in the IDToken itself, you can use
the `traits` field to populate the identity's traits. Note, that Apple only includes the users email in the IDToken.

Supported providers are
Apple
Google

id_token_nonce.description:
IDTokenNonce is the nonce, used when generating the IDToken.
If the provider supports nonce validation, the nonce will be validated against this value and required.

method.description:
Method to use

This field must be set to `oidc` when using the oidc method.

traits.description: The identity traits. This is a placeholder for the registration flow.

transient_payload.description: Transient data to pass along to any webhooks

upstream_parameters.description:
UpstreamParameters are the parameters that are passed to the upstream identity provider.

These parameters are optional and depend on what the upstream identity provider supports.
Supported parameters are:
`login_hint` (string): The `login_hint` parameter suppresses the account chooser and either pre-fills the email box on the sign-in form, or selects the proper session.
`hd` (string): The `hd` parameter limits the login/registration process to a Google Organization, e.g. `mycollege.edu`.
`prompt` (string): The `prompt` specifies whether the Authorization Server prompts the End-User for reauthentication and consent, e.g. `select_account`.
`acr_values` (string): The `acr_values` specifies the Authentication Context Class Reference values for the authorization request.

oidc.title: updateLoginFlowWithOidcMethod

passkey.description: Update Login Flow with Passkey Method

csrf_token.description: Sending the anti-csrf token is only required for browser login flows.

method.description: Method should be set to "passkey" when logging in using the Passkey strategy.

passkey_login.description:
Login a WebAuthn Security Key

This must contain the ID of the WebAuthN connection.

passkey.title: updateLoginFlowWithPasskeyMethod

password.description: Update Login Flow with Password Method

csrf_token.description: Sending the anti-csrf token is only required for browser login flows.

identifier.description: Identifier is the email or username of the user trying to log in.

method.description: Method should be set to "password" when logging in using the identifier and password strategy.

password_identifier.description:

This field is deprecated!

transient_payload.description: Transient data to pass along to any webhooks
